name: Pastel Tres Leches Aldaco
recipeIngredient: Or cinnamon to sprinkle on top
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Lightly grease a 9-inch round cake pan.
3. Mix together flour, baking powder, sugar, and salt in a medium bowl.
4. In another bowl, stir eggs, milk, and vanilla lightly.
5. Mix the egg mixture with the dry ingredients and stir well.
6. Pour the batter into the prepared cake pan and bake for about 20 minutes or until the top is light golden and springs back when touched in the center.
7. Remove from oven and let cool for 15 minutes.
8. Remove from pan so it doesn't stick. Let cool completely.
9. When cooled, cut horizontally with a long-bladed knife.
10. To make milk mixture: combine evaporated milk, sweetened condensed milk, whole milk, vanilla (if desired), and rum (optional) in a large mixing bowl or blender. Blend well.
11. Lift the top half of the cake and set aside.
12. Place the bottom half on a larger serving dish with a rim to catch excess milk.
13. Pour enough milk mixture over the bottom half until well saturated.
14. Replace the top half and carefully pour more milk mixture over the top.
15. Allow the cake to soak and chill in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours (better if soaked overnight).
16. Cut into pie-shaped wedges and garnish with chopped pecans or a sprinkle of cinnamon on top.
17. Any leftover milk can be poured into a small pitcher and served alongside the dessert.
From Aldaco's restaurant in San Antonio, Texas. Absolutely delicious!
